steps.title: Establish the confidence interval from margin of error
steps.content: The survey estimates 40% support with a margin of error of 4%. This means the true percentage supporting the ordinance is plausibly between 40% - 4% = 36% and 40% + 4% = 44%.

steps.title: Convert percentage bounds to population counts
steps.content:
The population is 60,000 people. Apply the percentage bounds:
Lower bound: 0.36 x 60,000 = 21,600
Upper bound: 0.44 x 60,000 = 26,400
So a plausible value for the number of supporters falls between 21,600 and 26,400 people.

steps.title: Check which choice falls in the plausible range
steps.content:
Test each option:
  • $480$: 480 -> represents 0.8% of the population, far too small
  • $720$: 720 -> represents 1.2% of the population, far too small
  • $24,600$: 24,600 -> represents 41% of 60,000, which lies within [36%, 44%]
  • $35,400$: 35,400 -> represents 59% of the population, far too large
That matches the correct answer - C is correct.
